title: Plans and Bundles for User Licensing and Upgrades
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want the ability to upgrade to a paid plan from within the application so that I can add more users and expand my account's data sharing capacity.
description:
Spike and initial implementation for a plans/bundles system that controls user licensing, shared user limits, and trial expiration at the account level.

A new `plans` model was created with fields: `user_id`, `name`, `type` (free/trial/monthly/yearly), `account_member_quantity`, `price`, `stripe_plan_id`, `status` (active/inactive), and `description`. Every authenticated user receives a free plan by default.

A Laravel middleware enforces the `account_member_quantity` limit — when a user attempts to invite more users than their plan allows, an error is shown. The middleware guards all invitation POST requests, preventing bypass even if the UI section is hidden.

Additional data points were requested: `createddate` and `inactivedate` timestamps for audit purposes, plus `crm_id` and `license_id` foreign keys linking to external CRM plan and license tables.
acceptance_criteria: Plans model exists with fields for name, type, account_member_quantity, price, status, and description
Free plan is automatically created for each new authenticated user
Middleware enforces the account_member_quantity limit on invitation requests
Users receive an error message when they exceed the allowed number of shared users
Middleware guards POST requests for invitations even if the UI is bypassed
Plan records include createddate and inactivedate timestamps
Plan records include crm_id and license_id foreign keys for CRM integration
ux_changes: Error message displayed when a user attempts to invite more users than their plan allows.
middleware_changes: New Laravel middleware that checks the user's plan account_member_quantity before allowing invitation requests. Blocks and returns an error if the limit is reached.
data_model_changes: New plans table with columns: user_id, name, type (free/trial/monthly/yearly), account_member_quantity, price, stripe_plan_id, status, description, createddate, inactivedate, crm_id (FK), license_id (FK).
implementation_notes: Stripe integration is planned for the future using Laravel Cashier. The stripe_plan_id field is a placeholder. The middleware approach ensures security even if the UI invitation section is hidden. Laravel Cashier can create plans on Stripe with trial, monthly, and yearly configurations.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the plans model with a free default plan and middleware enforcement for user limits. Product requested additional audit timestamps (createddate, inactivedate) and foreign keys (crm_id, license_id) for CRM integration.
